prompt: Write four absurd but coherent sentences in the voice of a confused medieval chronicler who has been hired to document the Q3 results of a modern SaaS company.
response:
Forsooth, in this third quarter of our Lord's year, the scrolls of the cloud have doth yielded a bounty of 273 new chariots, pulled by the horses of Microsoft. 
Verily, the jousting tournament of ARR has championed growth, with a lance that pierced through the hearts of many a foe, and emerged victorious with a sum of $97.7 million.
Oh, the revelry and fanfare as the knights of customer retention donned their shields with a motion most graceful, boasting an NRR of 107% like the dragon slaying days of olde. 
But alas, the alchemy of Free Cash Flow hath transmogrified into a concoction that would make even the most skilled of potion brewers scratch their head in confoundment, a mere $907,000.
